detail.title: Report Output, Triage / Resolution UX, Decisions, Persistence

consumes.artifact: Structured finding lists returned by the three sub-agents
AskUserQuestion tool (Claude Code deferred tool; max 4 questions per call, max 4 options per question, "Other" auto-appended)

produces.artifact: Main-session terminal markdown (Summary table, Findings Overview table, per-finding Detail blocks, AskUserQuestion flows, Decisions table)

requirement.title: Phase 2 markdown report with mandatory table layout
requirement.description: Phase 2 MUST render three distinct sections of markdown output — Summary table, Findings Overview table, and per-finding Detail blocks — before proceeding to triage. Tables carry as much content as possible; prose is reserved only for "why it matters" paragraphs. When exactly one HIGH-severity finding exists in the overall finding set, its row in the Findings Overview MUST carry a visual emphasis marker so the reader's eye catches it even when no Phase 3 triage call fires (the below-threshold case governed by fr-oi-002).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given all three sub-agents have returned (or returned partial data with error flags), When Phase 2 executes, Then the main session MUST print a Summary markdown table whose columns are axis, HIGH count, MED count, LOW count, INFO count, and Total, with one row per axis plus a totals row. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Output format

Given Phase 2 is running, When the Findings Overview is printed, Then it MUST be a markdown table with columns ID / Sev / Axis / Title / primary file, containing one row per finding across all three axes. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Output format

Given Phase 2 is running, When per-finding Detail blocks are printed, Then the Observed evidence MUST be rendered as a markdown table (columns such as location / statement or location / evidence), and Suggested Resolutions MUST be rendered as a markdown table whose rows enumerate each resolution with its label, description, and (when applicable) side-effect or cost column. Only the "why it matters" paragraph MAY remain free prose. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Output format (clarified in design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Context — Defect 2: only Observed and Suggested Resolutions are tables; why-it-matters stays prose)

Given findings include MED, LOW, or INFO severity, When Phase 2 renders, Then those findings MUST appear in the Findings Overview table and MUST have their Detail blocks printed, regardless of whether they are later selected into triage. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Output format

Given the audit produces exactly one HIGH-severity finding across the full finding set, When the Findings Overview table is rendered, Then the row for that HIGH finding MUST start its Title column with a ⚠️ prefix AND render the Title text in markdown bold, so the single HIGH is visually distinguished from lower-severity rows even when Phase 3 does not fire. design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1 (N_HIGH == 1 visual emphasis)

requirement.title: Triage UX — conditional multi-select over HIGH findings with free-text pull-in
requirement.description: Phase 3 MUST fire only when the audit produced at least 2 HIGH-severity findings; the AskUserQuestion tool's `options.minItems: 2` constraint makes a single-option triage call illegal, and a zero-HIGH call has no content. When Phase 3 fires, it MUST use AskUserQuestion with `multiSelect: true` to let the user pick which HIGH findings proceed into Stage 2 resolution; MED, LOW, and INFO findings MUST NOT appear in triage options, but the user MAY inject specific IDs (of any severity) via the auto-appended Other free-text option. When Phase 3 does not fire (N_HIGH < 2), the skill takes the degraded paths defined by ac5 and ac6 — Phase 2 Detail blocks are the deliverable for any sub-HIGH follow-up, and no alternative injection channel is provided.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given Phase 3 is entered AND the audit produced at least 2 HIGH-severity findings, When the first AskUserQuestion call is made, Then it MUST set `multiSelect: true`, `header: "Triage"`, and list up to 4 HIGH-severity findings as `options` in a stable order (earliest HIGH by axis-then-ID first). design.md §Architecture — Phase 3 Triage (conditional firing added in design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1)

Given the audit produced more than 4 HIGH findings, When Phase 3 runs, Then additional sequential AskUserQuestion calls MUST be issued, each carrying up to 4 not-yet-offered HIGH findings, until every HIGH finding has been presented to the user exactly once across the triage phase. Refiner clarifying question Q3 → answer (a): sequential multi-batch triage

Given Phase 3 is firing (N_HIGH >= 2) AND the user uses the auto-appended "Other" option in any triage call to submit free-text containing one or more finding IDs (format `A<axis>-<NNN>`, any severity — including MED, LOW, or INFO), When the skill parses the Other input, Then the specified IDs MUST be added to the Stage 2 resolution queue, even when their severity is below HIGH. Refiner clarifying question Q2 → answer (b): Other may inject MED/LOW IDs (precondition tightened in design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1: Other pull-in only exists when Phase 3 actually fires)

Given Phase 3 is running, When any AskUserQuestion call is issued during triage, Then MED, LOW, and INFO findings MUST NOT appear in that call's `options` list; users may only reach them via the Other free-text channel. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Triage UX

Given the audit produced zero HIGH-severity findings, When the skill finishes Phase 2, Then the skill MUST NOT issue any Phase 3 AskUserQuestion call, MUST NOT enter Phase 4, MUST NOT render a Phase 5 Decisions table, MUST print a concluding recommendation line referring the user to the Phase 2 Detail blocks for any follow-up, AND MUST exit cleanly with a successful status. design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1 (N_HIGH == 0 exit path)

Given the audit produced exactly one HIGH-severity finding, When the skill finishes Phase 2, Then the skill MUST NOT issue any Phase 3 multi-select AskUserQuestion call (which would violate `options.minItems: 2`), MUST ensure the Phase 2 Findings Overview row for that HIGH carries the visual emphasis required by fr-oi-001-ac5, AND MUST proceed directly into Phase 4 with that single HIGH finding as the sole Stage-2 queue entry — subject thereafter to fr-oi-003's per-finding resolution-count rules, which may further collapse Phase 4 into a no-question case. design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1 (N_HIGH == 1 direct-to-Phase-4 flow)

requirement.title: Resolution UX — conditional batched per-finding AskUserQuestion with preview diffs
requirement.description: Phase 4 MUST iterate the Stage-2 queue in batches of at most 4 AskUserQuestion questions per call, rendering one question per selected finding that has at least 2 suggested resolutions. Findings with fewer than 2 suggested resolutions MUST NOT have an AskUserQuestion question issued for them — AskUserQuestion's `options.minItems: 2` constraint forbids a single-option question, and asking among a single option is pure ceremony. Such findings rely on their Phase 2 Detail block's Suggested Resolutions table as the deliverable and appear in the Decisions table (when it is rendered) with a sentinel value. Resolutions with editable-artifact equivalents MUST include `preview` diff content; resolutions without such an equivalent MAY omit `preview`.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given Phase 4 starts with a Stage-2 queue whose M entries each have at least 2 suggested resolutions, When AskUserQuestion questions are issued, Then each call MUST contain at most 4 questions; sequential calls MUST be used until all M qualifying findings have been asked exactly once. design.md §Architecture — Phase 4 Resolution (resolution-count precondition added in design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1)

Given one question is rendered for a finding, When its structure is inspected, Then `header` MUST be the finding ID (≤12 characters), `question` MUST include the finding title plus a compact observed summary, `options` MUST contain up to 4 reviewer-suggested resolutions, and `multiSelect` MUST be `false`. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Resolution UX

Given a resolution corresponds to an editable-artifact change, When its option is rendered, Then the option MUST include a `preview` field carrying a diff snippet representing the post-apply state. Resolutions that recommend external action with no direct editable equivalent (e.g., "patch coordinator.js" engine fixes) MAY omit `preview`. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Resolution UX

Given the reviewer marked a recommended resolution on a finding, When that finding's options are rendered, Then the first option's `label` MUST start with `"(Recommended)"`. design.md §Requirements Part 1 — Finding structure

Given every resolution question is rendered, When AskUserQuestion inserts its auto-appended Other option, Then the skill MUST accept the user's free-text Other response as a valid decision equivalent to the listed resolutions, without error. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Resolution UX

Given the Stage-2 resolution queue contains a finding whose suggested-resolutions count is less than 2, When Phase 4 iterates to that finding, Then the skill MUST NOT issue any AskUserQuestion question for it, MUST (when the Decisions table is rendered per fr-oi-004) record that finding's row with Chosen Resolution set to the sentinel string `(no ceremony — see Detail)` and User Note empty, AND MUST NOT treat the skip as an error. design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1 (single-resolution finding skipped at Phase 4, sentinel recorded in Decisions table)

requirement.title: Decisions table output at skill end (conditional on ceremony firing)
requirement.description: Phase 5's Decisions markdown table MUST be rendered only when Phase 3 or Phase 4 actually fired during this invocation — i.e., when there was ceremony producing decisions to record. When both Phase 3 and Phase 4 were skipped (the N_HIGH == 0 path governed by fr-oi-002-ac5), no Decisions table MUST be printed; the skill exits after the Phase 2 report plus the concluding recommendation line. When the Decisions table is rendered, it summarises the user's choices across all discussed findings, including auto-skipped single-resolution findings recorded via the sentinel defined in fr-oi-003-ac6. The skill body MUST NOT perform any Edit or Write on audited artifacts based on those decisions — post-audit execution is explicitly the main session's responsibility.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given Phase 3 or Phase 4 actually fired during this invocation AND those phases completed and collected user answers, When Phase 5 runs, Then the main session MUST print a markdown Decisions table with columns Finding ID / Chosen Resolution / User Note. design.md §Architecture — Phase 5 Decisions (conditional firing added in design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1)

Given the user submitted free-text via AskUserQuestion's Other option on any question, When the Decisions table row for that finding is populated, Then the User Note column MUST contain that free-text verbatim; when no Other text was provided, the User Note column MUST be empty. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Decisions

Given Phase 5 completes, When the skill exits, Then the skill body MUST NOT invoke Edit, Write, or any subsequent artifact-mutating action based on user decisions — main session ownership of any subsequent actions is explicit and out of scope for this skill. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Decisions

Given both Phase 3 and Phase 4 were skipped during this invocation per fr-oi-002-ac5 (the N_HIGH == 0 path), When the skill reaches its exit point, Then no Decisions table MUST be printed; the skill MUST exit cleanly after the Phase 2 report and the concluding recommendation line, with no Phase 5 output rendered. design 2026-04-24-iterate2 §Change Intent — Change 1 (Decisions table conditional on ceremony firing)

requirement.title: Optional report persistence via --save flag
requirement.description: The skill MUST NOT write any file by default. When the user supplies `--save`, the full report plus the Decisions table MUST be written to a canonical path under the arcforge home directory, using the existing worktree-path hash algorithm for the project-scoping segment.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given the user invokes `/arc-auditing-spec <id>` without the `--save` flag, When the skill ends (successful completion or partial-failure completion), Then no file MUST be written to disk anywhere. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Persistence

Given the user invokes `/arc-auditing-spec <id> --save`, When Phase 5 completes, Then a markdown file containing the full Phase 2 report plus the Phase 5 Decisions table MUST be written to `~/.arcforge/reviews/<project-hash>/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM>.md`. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Persistence

Given the `<project-hash>` segment of the persistence path is computed, When the skill assembles the path, Then the hash derivation MUST reuse the existing algorithm used by arcforge's worktree-path computation (currently in `scripts/lib/worktree-paths.js`), such that a single project maps to a single hash across this skill's review paths and existing arcforge worktree-state paths. design.md §Requirements Part 2 — Persistence
